title: fix: correlate provider-executed tool results back to tool calls

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: feat: support Anthropic programmatic tool calling (PTC)
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: supersedes — PR #12400 merged on 2026-03-10 and includes the identical fix: correlating provider-executed tool results to tool calls via toolCallId in llm-execution-step.ts

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(core): prevent stream bail for provider-executed tools called in parallel
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: related — addresses provider-executed tool handling in the same area of code

relatedIssues.title: Provider-executed tool results overwritten by fallback sentinel in agentic loop

summary: This PR adds 15 lines to llm-execution-step.ts to correlate provider-executed tool results (from _getImmediateToolResults()) back to their tool-call entries by toolCallId, preventing the loss of actual output data (stdout, stderr, etc.) when provider-executed tools like Anthropic's code_execution reach tool-call-step. A changeset is included. However, the exact same fix was already merged as part of PR #12400 (commit b2204c98a4, merged 2026-03-10), which restructures the toolCalls construction to merge tool-result chunks inline. The PR's branch is based on the older code and would produce a duplicate/conflicting implementation.
maintainerNotes: This PR is superseded by #12400 which was merged today. The fix in #12400 (lines 1051-1066 of the current llm-execution-step.ts) does exactly what this PR proposes — correlating tool results to provider-executed tool calls by toolCallId — but in a cleaner inline style. This PR can be closed. Issue #13846 is still open and should be closed as fixed by #12400. Thank the author for the contribution and point them to #12400.
